mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Grafana instances and their resources on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer to manage their observability stack using Kubernetes-native tools and GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing custom resources versus traditional deployment methods.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Grafana Operator Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using the specified version.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a custom resource definition (CRD) that the Grafana Operator manages.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Displays the detailed status of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator from your cluster. Be aware that this will also delete all Grafana instances and associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the Grafana instance and its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backs up the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Grafana instance is running with the new version.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator for errors: kubectl logs -l app=grafana-operator

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the Grafana instance status: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not be correctly configured for the Grafana Operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure the necessary roles and role bindings are set up for the Grafana Operator.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to retrieve Grafana dashboards'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The GrafanaDashboard CRD may not be correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Check the GrafanaDashboard CRD configuration: kubectl get grafanadashboard -o yaml

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' when executing: kubectl get grafana grafana.

spec:

    security:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
